player_notes.joel embiid: Returned from injury and played Game 3 vs. Knicks; availability confirmed but Sixers still lost 108-94. Monitor minutes/effectiveness heading into potential Game 4.
player_notes.og anunoby: OUT for Game 3 vs. 76ers; status for Game 4 unknown. Absence affects Knicks wing rotation and could elevate usage for other Knicks role players.
player_notes.jalen brunson: 33 points in Game 3 win; primary offensive engine with Anunoby out. High-volume role likely continues in potential closeout Game 4.
player_notes.victor wembanyama: 39 pts, 15 reb in Game 3 win over Wolves; elite production trend continues. No injury concerns, dominant role confirmed.
player_notes.shai gilgeous-alexander: Dealt with foul trouble in Game 2 vs. Lakers; Thunder still won. Foul trouble risk is a minutes/production concern if it recurs in Game 3.
player_notes.chet holmgren: 22 points in Game 2 with SGA limited by fouls; elevated role/usage when SGA sits. Worth targeting if foul trouble pattern continues.
player_notes.cade cunningham: 25 pts, 10 ast in Game 2 win; leading Pistons with 2-0 series lead over Cavs. High-usage, high-confidence target.
player_notes.james harden: 3-for-13 FG, 0-for-4 from 3 in Game 2 loss; two straight poor playoff games. Significant concern for points and 3PM props.

game_notes.NYK vs PHI: OG Anunoby (OUT) missing for Knicks; Joel Embiid returned for Sixers in Game 3. Embiid's presence boosts PHI big man props; Anunoby absence reshapes NYK wing minutes.
game_notes.SAS vs MIN: No player availability issues reported. Wembanyama in dominant form; Wolves facing 2-1 deficit with pressure to respond in Game 4.
